title: Daily Source Pass
summary: Today's issue is built from public and approved sources plus first-class supervised community lanes: official Metro pages, local reporting, direct venue/business pages, library hours, Facebook group capture, and Nextdoor capture.

stages.name: Official Records Sweep
stages.goal: Find the safest, highest-confidence civic items before the brief goes out.
stages.output: Published the road-safety / 311 item and East Bank Boulevard watch; keep NDOT permits and 311 clustering in the daily scan.

stages.name: Local Web And Meeting Sweep
stages.goal: Catch Nashville-wide items that need an East Nashville translation.
stages.output: Published the Starbucks incentive watch and Metro event-money meeting brief with source credit.

stages.name: Direct Pages And Community Temperature
stages.goal: Add same-day utility from venues, businesses, libraries, and supervised community chatter.
stages.output: Published direct-source event, library, and restaurant-check items; queued Facebook and Nextdoor as daily supervised capture lanes until selected posts have internal notes or screenshots.

stages.name: Publish Pack
stages.goal: Turn the source sweep into live stories, newsletter blocks, social copy, and follow-up prompts.
stages.output: Eight source-backed public items are live for May 12; Facebook and Nextdoor remain active supervised capture slots until real posts are selected.

publishQueue.decision: Published as today's source issue lead.
publishQueue.why: It explains what the public scrape found, what got published, and how Facebook/Nextdoor join the run through selected capture instead of raw private scraping.

publishQueue.decision: Published as a reported incentive-watch story.
publishQueue.why: The dollar figure and vote date are useful Nashville public-money context with follow-up value.

publishQueue.decision: Published as the road-safety / hubNashville irritation item.
publishQueue.why: It turns neighborhood complaints into a source-backed public-service workflow.

publishQueue.decision: Published as the tourism-money meeting brief.
publishQueue.why: Visitor-economy decisions can become East Nashville traffic, East Bank, and event impacts.

publishQueue.decision: Published as an East Bank Boulevard watch item.
publishQueue.why: This is the riverfront infrastructure piece East Nashville should keep tracking.

publishQueue.decision: Published as a same-day event quick hit.
publishQueue.why: Direct venue pages are low-risk, useful, and immediate.

publishQueue.decision: Published as a practical late-hours library item.
publishQueue.why: Useful public infrastructure deserves a place in the brief.

publishQueue.decision: Published as a stale-source correction and active-business check.
publishQueue.why: It demonstrates the restaurant scrape rule: search result first, direct source before publication.

publishQueue.decision: Daily supervised Facebook capture lane is active; publish themes or verified follow-ups after selected posts have source notes.
publishQueue.why: The group is a core neighborhood signal source, but private chatter needs transformation and verification before publication.

publishQueue.decision: Daily supervised Nextdoor capture lane is active through the Source Desk panel; paste or open selected posts before drafting.
publishQueue.why: Nextdoor belongs in the same source pass as public records, but its private-citizen content needs stricter handling.

accessNeeds.name: Facebook private group
accessNeeds.today: Open or paste the 5-10 posts worth reviewing, plus screenshots or source notes, then convert patterns into labeled drafts.
accessNeeds.guardrail: No unattended private-group scraping. Publish themes or verified follow-ups, not raw private accusations.

accessNeeds.name: Nextdoor
accessNeeds.today: Paste selected neighborhood notes into the Source Desk panel and use them for patterns: lost-and-found, services, family logistics, safety chatter, and rumor prompts.
accessNeeds.guardrail: Do not name private citizens or publish accusations without public confirmation.

accessNeeds.name: Public official and local web sources
accessNeeds.today: Keep the May 12 source issue live and rerun the scan for follow-up records, agendas, minutes, and direct updates.
accessNeeds.guardrail: Credit outlets, link official pages, and clearly distinguish reported, direct, and confirmed source labels.

accessNeeds.name: Instagram/X watchlists
accessNeeds.today: Seed direct business, venue, Metro, reporter, food writer, and local organization accounts before treating these as a full daily scrape.
accessNeeds.guardrail: Direct posts can confirm their own facts; third-party comments remain leads until verified.
